description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical stone base and a pitched wooden roof supported by four wooden posts. The stonework is rugged and uneven, giving the well an aged, rustic appearance. A wooden crossbeam supports a hand-crank wheel and a rope, which is attached to a small wooden bucket for drawing water. The roof is made of overlapping wooden planks, providing shelter to the well opening and the pulley mechanism. The interior of the well appears hollow and dark, suggesting depth for water storage. The overall construction is robust, with visible wood grain and stone texture, suitable for outdoor urban or park environments.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden roof, hand-crank pulley, and bucket, featuring detailed stone and wood textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched wooden roof, hand-crank wheel, rope, and bucket clearly above the stone well opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered wooden roof and the circular stone wall, with the pulley mechanism partially obscured.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the wooden roof, crank wheel, and bucket, but from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the wooden roof and stone wall, with the pulley and bucket less visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof made of overlapping planks, supported by wooden posts; no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible hand-crank wheel with rope and wooden bucket suspended above the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, forming a protective barrier around the well opening; wooden roof structure overhead.
